context_pointers.vague_reference: use the same behavior as C025 but omit the alg field
context_pointers.actual_meaning: The system outputs a JWT Key Set JSON object. If the 'with_alg' parameter is explicitly NOT specified for a 'secret' kind JWK, it will output the object without the 'alg' key, defaulting only to kty='oct' and use='sig'.

context_pointers.vague_reference: export the EC key with alg ES256 by default
context_pointers.actual_meaning: The system outputs a JWT Key Set JSON object. When the key is EC private and keys are exported via 'to_jwk', the system defaults to adding 'alg':'ES256' if not explicitly overridden, determined by the implied curve length of the key material.

oracle_data.fallback_response: I don't have more detail on that — check the codebase or the existing test cases for the exact behavior.

feature1_1_sign_hmac.json.description: Issue a compact token from a map payload using an HMAC shared-secret algorithm; the output is the full compact serialization (header.payload.signature).

feature1_2_sign_rsa_pkcs1.json.description: Issue a compact token from a map payload using an RSA PKCS#1 v1.5 private key; deterministic signature.

feature1_3_sign_ecdsa.json.description: Issue a compact token from a map payload using an elliptic-curve private key (deterministic ECDSA) across the P-256, P-384, P-521 and secp256k1 curves.

key.pem:
-----BEGIN EC PRIVATE KEY-----
MHQCAQEEINCRiJnNDnzfo2So2tWY4AIuzeC2ZBp/hmMDcZz3Fh45oAcGBSuBBAAK
oUQDQgAE0aELkvG/Xeo5y6o0WXRAjlediLptGz7Q8zjDmpGFXkKBYZ6IiL7JJ2Tk
cHzd83bmeUeGX33RGTYFPXs5t/VBnw==
-----END EC PRIVATE KEY-----

feature1_4_sign_eddsa.json.description: Issue a compact token from a map payload using an Edwards-curve (Ed25519) private key.

feature2_1_verify_hmac.json.description: Validate an HMAC-signed compact token with the shared secret and return the embedded algorithm and claims.

feature2_2_verify_rsa_pkcs1.json.description: Validate an RSA PKCS#1 v1.5 signed compact token with the RSA public key and return algorithm and claims.

feature2_3_verify_ecdsa.json.description: Validate an ECDSA signed compact token with the matching public key across P-256, P-384, P-521 and secp256k1.

feature2_4_verify_rsa_pss.json.description: Validate an RSA-PSS signed compact token with the RSA public key and return algorithm and claims.

feature2_5_verify_eddsa.json.description: Validate an Ed25519 signed compact token with the Edwards-curve public key and return algorithm and claims.

feature3_1_exp.json.description: Enforce the expiration (exp) claim during verification: a token past its expiry is rejected, a token within validity returns its claims, and expiry checking can be switched off.

feature3_2_nbf.json.description: Enforce the not-before (nbf) claim during verification: a token not yet active is rejected, an already-active token returns its claims, and the not-before check can be switched off.

feature3_3_issue_at.json.description: Enforce an issued-at lower bound during verification: with a minimum issued-at threshold the token passes when its iat is at or after the threshold and is rejected when its iat predates it; with no threshold a future iat is accepted.

feature4_payload_types.json.description: Accept string and map payloads when issuing a token but reject a numeric payload as an invalid payload type.

feature5_1_export_jwk.json.description: Export a key to its JWK JSON object. Secret and RSA keys omit the algorithm hint unless requested; elliptic-curve and Edwards-curve keys always include their algorithm.

feature5_2_import_jwk.json.description: Import a JWK JSON object and report the resolved key category and key type for secret, RSA, elliptic-curve and Edwards-curve public and private keys.

_constraints.output_rule: Dispatch behavior based on exact 'op' value; unknown op returns parse_error
Requires algorithm, key, payload, no_iat. Missing fields treated as parse_error or validation failure
Requires token, key, and optionally check_exp, check_nbf, issue_at
Key object with 'kind' and either 'secret' (string) or 'pem' (multi-line string)
Key object with 'kind' and 'pem'; HMAC uses 'secret' string instead of pem
Accepted 'kind' values: 'secret', 'rsa_private', 'rsa_public', 'ec_private', 'ec_public', 'ed_private', 'ed_public'
Payload must be object or string. Numeric payloads (e.g. 1234) trigger invalid_payload_type error
Accepts string payload; encodes it for signing (base64url will contain the string bytes)
Algorithm must be standard JWT identifier; invalid alg in verify header or input algorithm triggers error
Produces exact token: top.signed.payload

_constraints.input_condition: op=sign, algorithm=RS256, specific PEM key payload={"foo":"bar"}
_constraints.output_rule: Produces exact token: top.signed.payload (PKCS#1 v1.5 deterministic)

_constraints.input_condition: op=sign, algorithm=ES256, specific PEM key payload={"foo":"bar"}
_constraints.output_rule: Produces exact token: top.signed.payload (RFC 6979 deterministic)

_constraints.input_condition: op=sign, algorithm=ES256K, specific PEM key payload={"foo":"bar"}
_constraints.output_rule: Produces exact token; header differs with 'kty':'cek'

_constraints.input_condition: op=sign, algorithm=EdDSA, specific PEM key payload={"foo":"bar"}
_constraints.output_rule: Produces exact token (Ed25519)
Exact output structure: line 1 'alg=X'; line 2 'payload={JSON}'
Exact output structure; payload sorted keys
Exact output structure
Returns error=token_expired
Returns error=token_not_active
Returns error=invalid_token with reason=expired_issue_at
Ignores exp check; returns alg and payload even if expired
Ignores nbf check; returns alg and payload even if not active
No check on iat; returns payload
Default output without 'alg' field unless 'with_alg' is specified
Includes 'alg' as first key in object
Always includes 'alg' field based on key material implied

_constraints.input_condition: op=from_jwk, jwk contains k, kty, use
_constraints.output_rule: Returns kind=secret, kty=oct

_constraints.input_condition: op=from_jwk, jwk has 'd', 'dp', 'dq' fields; no 'd' (c) indicates using witness logic or standard RSA fields
_constraints.output_rule: Determined as kind=rsa_private

_constraints.input_condition: op=from_jwk, jwk contains d (private), x, y
_constraints.output_rule: Determined as kind=ec_private

_constraints.input_condition: op=from_jwk, jwk contains d, x with alg=EdDSA
_constraints.output_rule: Determined as kind=ed_private
Output ends with exactly one newline character
Line 1: 'error=<category>'. Line 2: 'reason=<slug>' (if applicable). Sorted payload keys

_constraints.input_condition: Token valid signature but claim check (exp/nbf/iat) fails
_constraints.output_rule: Reason cannot be negative or positive, must be Expired, not active, or Invalid Format (could not be parsed or rejected)
JSON object keys sorted alphabetically
Standard base64url encoding (no padding =), safe characters - and _.
Extracts raw 'k' value as 'secret' string if kty is oct
Maps 'd' (private) or 'x'+sync data to kind=... and kty=...; 'n' without kty specified maps to kind=rsa_private and kty=rsa_public
PEM-encoded keys provided as multiline string starting with -----BEGIN
JSON serialization with keys sorted
Extracts key algorithm if 'alg' field is provided or implied (e.g. EC/EdRSA curves)
Extracts 'alg' from header of token; verify that it matches the provided key's capabilities
If 'alg' present, must be string
Valid values: oct, rsa, ec, okp
Valid values: sig
Returns error=invalid_token reason=invalid_format
Returns error=invalid_token reason=invalid_signature
Payload must contain 'iat' claim if not suppressed
Payload must include 'iat' claim
Returns error=token_expired if 'exp' is before current unix timestamp
Returns error=token_not_active if 'nbf' is after current unix timestamp
Returns error=invalid_token reason=expired_issue_at
Returns error=token_expired (treated as expired if iat is in past relative to issue_at)
Extracts 'k' string
Extracts 'k' for secret; 'n' for public key
Output must be valid JSON
Extracts 'k' for secret, 'n' for public key
Requires RSA public key with PS256 capability

_constraints.input_condition: op=sign, algorithm=ES384 or ES512 or ES256K
_constraints.output_rule: Requires corresponding P-384, P-521, or secp256k1 key
Line 1: 'error=<category>'; Line 2: 'reason=<slug>'
Header must include 'alg' and 'typ':'JWT'
Payload must exclude 'iat' claim
Payload values base64url encoded without padding
Returns stringified JSON with sorted keys
Returns 'alg=<algorithm>' string
Outputs 'kind' and 'kty' only; raw 'k' string in second line
